seoTitle: Add a New Page in Webflow
metaDescription: How to create a new page in Webflow's Designer, set its URL slug, and configure SEO and Open Graph settings before publishing.
intro: Expanding your site with a new landing page, services tab, or contact form starts with Webflow's Pages panel. This guide covers creating a fresh page, naming its URL slug, and filling in the SEO fields that control how it looks in search results and social shares.

steps.heading: Open the Pages panel
steps.body: Click the Pages icon in the top left control panel to expand the site tree layout.

steps.heading: Create a new page
steps.body: Click the plus icon in the upper right of the panel to open the Create New Page form.

steps.heading: Name your page
steps.body: Type a descriptive, clean title, then review and customize the auto-generated URL slug to keep it short and relevant.

steps.heading: Fill in SEO and Open Graph fields
steps.body: Scroll to the SEO settings to enter a meta title and description, then expand Open Graph to control how the page appears when shared on social apps.

steps.heading: Create and start building
steps.body: Click the Create button in the top right to launch your blank page, then confirm it appears in the Pages tray before adding elements.

faq.question: Can I change the URL slug after creating the page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit the slug anytime in the page settings, though changing it after publishing may affect existing links to that page.

faq.question: Do I need to fill in the SEO fields right away?
faq.answer: It's best practice to set them before publishing, since search engines and social platforms pull from those fields when the page goes live.

faq.question: Can I add tracking scripts to just one page?
faq.answer: Yes, the page-specific Custom Code section lets you paste header or footer scripts that only apply to that individual page.

faq.question: How should I organize pages if my site has a lot of them?
faq.answer: Use folders in the Pages panel to group related pages together and keep your domain's URL structure clean.
